title: The Fused Agent Kernel (one page)
caption: The Fused Agent Kernel: one Go binary collapses four subsystems that are separate today (inference engine, local harness, fleet, and DOS) into a single OS. The model lives in untrusted userspace at the top, and every proposed tool call is a syscall that must pass DOWN through the kernel's syscall layer (typed Tool Table ABI, Tool vDSO, Pre-flight Verification Ladder, the DOS Reference Monitor adjudicating from evidence the agent did not author, an io_uring queue with speculative execution, and the Context MMU admitting results) before it can reach the side-effecting World, with the engine/scheduler beneath and 10x Stewards plus the RSI keep-or-revert loop wrapping the whole binary.
altText: A top-down OS-style diagram showing an untrusted model at the top whose tool calls flow down as syscalls through a kernel syscall layer (Tool Table, vDSO, verification ladder, DOS reference monitor, io_uring queue, Context MMU) and an engine/scheduler with a KV-cache hierarchy, all inside one Go binary, with Stewards and an RSI loop wrapping it and side-effecting tools as the World at the bottom.
killerNumber: 4 subsystems -> 1 Go binary
issuesFound: Faithfulness: source says the binary fuses FOUR named subsystems (engine, harness, fleet, DOS), but the draft's box labels did not name them. Annotated the BIN box as 'ONE Go binary (engine + harness + fleet + DOS)', the SYS box as 'Syscall layer / kernel (harness + DOS)', and the ENG box as 'Engine / scheduler (fleet 2-D sched)' so all four subsystems are explicitly mapped to the OS layout.
Verified the killer number '4 subsystems -> 1 Go binary' is present as a note-class node (punch), rendered via the → entity which mermaid resolves.

Verified node count = 13 flow nodes (punch, model, tbl, vdso, ladder, mon, uring, mmu, batch, kv, stewards, rsi, world), within the <=14 limit; subgraphs BIN/SYS/ENG are containers, not counted nodes.

Confirmed required-include relationships are all drawn: model untrusted at top; tool call passes DOWN through syscall-layer/DOS (deny/require-witness loops back to model) before reaching world; engine/scheduler beneath; stewards + RSI loop wrapping BIN; single-binary subgraph box around engine + syscall-layer.

title: The boundaries ARE the cost
caption: Before/after of one tool call: separated today, the engine pays a serialized tax chain — serialize args, cross an IPC socket, spawn the DOS hook as a process (Go 5.4-13.9ms, Python 232-262ms), adjudicate, run, serialize back, then re-tokenize and re-prefill on a KV miss (50-100ms+); fused into one binary, it is zero-copy structs, a plain function call, in-process Go adjudication (38ns-6us), and a resident KV. The sub-10us adjudication logic was strangled by a per-call PROCESS BOUNDARY, and fusion deletes it: ~1-300 ms drops to ~tens of us, ~1000x on overhead.
altText: Two side-by-side flow columns comparing one tool call: the left "Separated (today)" column shows a long serialized chain with millisecond costs, the right "Fused (one binary)" column shows a short zero-copy in-process chain with microsecond costs, both pointing to a note reading ~1-300 ms to ~tens of us (~1000x on overhead).
killerNumber: ~1-300 ms -> ~tens of us (~1000x on overhead)
issuesFound: Verified faithfulness: every source number is present (serialize 50-200us, IPC 0.2-1ms, Go hook 5.4-13.9ms, Py hook 232-262ms, re-prefill 50-100ms+, in-proc adjudicate 38ns-6us / ~160us FS-probe, zero-copy ~0, KV 0 re-prefill) and the two-column Separated-vs-Fused structure with the ~1000x net label is intact.

Verified Mermaid syntax: first line is the exact required init block; classDef block matches verbatim; no node id is named 'end'; all labels containing ( ) [ ] { } / are double-quoted; all node shapes are balanced; all 14 nodes carry a class.

Replaced the literal '->' arrow text inside the punch-note label with the word 'to' (and prefixed 'Overhead:') so no stray '->' token can be misread by a strict parser inside the note; the killer number remains visible as the note-class node.

title: The tool call IS a system call
caption: The tool call IS a system call: each Agent Kernel concept (left) maps by a dashed equivalence to its classic OS mechanism (right) -- tool call == int 0x80, the typed Tool Table == the syscall table/ABI, the DOS Reference Monitor == seccomp-bpf + LSM + eBPF, the Tool vDSO == the vDSO, the pre-flight ladder == fail-fast, and context == the MMU. The verdict always comes from non-self-authored evidence, never the model's narration.
altText: A two-column diagram pairing six Agent Kernel concepts on the left with their equivalent classic OS mechanisms on the right via dashed equals links, framed by the keystone-reframe note above and a non-self-authored-verdict note below.
killerNumber: blow the thin 'tool call' up 100x into the agent OS syscall layer

Trimmed to the 6 most load-bearing rows (tool call, Tool Table, Reference Monitor, vDSO, pre-flight ladder, context) plus the 2 note nodes = 14 nodes, honoring both the <=14 cap and the source's 'pair the 6-8 most important rows' guidance.

Dropped the io_uring and speculative-execution pairs (a7/a8/o7/o8) as the least essential to the untrusted-process-asks-trusted-kernel thesis; updated the caption accordingly so it no longer claims those two rows are shown.
Removed the now-orphaned class assignments for a7,a8,o7,o8 (would have referenced non-existent nodes after the trim).

title: The life of one tool call
caption: The life of one tool call: the DOS Reference Monitor adjudicates a proposed call (allow/deny/transform/require-witness) on evidence the agent didn't author, the pre-flight ladder cheaply refutes losers without spending the call, dispatch answers pure/cached calls locally in the Tool vDSO or fires real ones via io_uring, and the Context Admission Gate either admits the result or quarantines it to a side store. Only bytes that earned their place ever touch context.
altText: A top-down flowchart tracing one tool call from the model's emission through adjudication, a pre-flight refute check, vDSO-versus-fire dispatch, and a context admission gate, with deny and quarantine off-ramps, ending at "enters context".
killerNumber: verdict from evidence the agent didn't author
issuesFound: Fixed a dangling class assignment: 'class vdso,fire2 kernel;' referenced node id 'fire2', which does not exist and would create a phantom node / risk a parse error. Changed to 'class vdso kernel;'.

Verified all 12 nodes are within the <=14 limit and every node is assigned exactly one class (emit=untrusted, adj/preflight/dispatch/gate=gate, deny/quarantine=deny, vdso=kernel, fire=world, sidestore=mem, ctx=pass, note=note).

Confirmed the killer number is present verbatim as a note-class node and dotted-linked to the adjudication diamond.
Verified init first line and classDef block match the required blocks verbatim, and all labels with special characters ( ) / , : are double-quoted; no node id named 'end'.

title: Pre-flight verification ladder (lint before pytest)
caption: The pre-flight verification ladder: before any tool call fires, run the cheapest mechanistic check that could prove it fails, climbing from rung 0 (static, microseconds) through rung 1 (dry-run / linter, milliseconds) and rung 2 (sandbox probe, tens of ms) to rung 3, which fires for real at full cost. Each rung can deny without spending the call, because a failed call's real cost is context pollution -- a ~500-2000 token stack trace plus a wasted recovery turn -- so it is worth spending 1 us to avoid a stack trace in context.
altText: A top-down four-rung ladder from an agent's proposed tool call through static, dry-run, and sandbox-probe gates to a final real fire, where each gate has a red deny off-ramp routing to the context-pollution cost note and the RSI labeled-example signal.
killerNumber: spend 1 us to avoid a stack trace in context
issuesFound: Verified faithfulness: all four rungs, their cost classes (microseconds / milliseconds / tens of ms / full cost), the three deny off-ramps, the RSI signal, the context-pollution punchline, and the killer number were all present and correct in the draft.
Clarified the RSI label from 'pass-k / fail-k+1' to 'pass-k / fail-(k+1)' so the off-by-one rung relationship reads unambiguously and the '+1' is not misread as a label artifact.
Added '(not wall-clock)' to the cost note to surface the source's explicit contrast (real cost is context pollution, not wall-clock time), strengthening the punchline.
Confirmed rung 3 correctly has no deny off-ramp: per the source, rungs 0-2 can DENY before spending while rung 3 IS the real spend, so only three deny off-ramps is faithful, not a missing element.

Confirmed syntax: required init block is the verbatim first line, exact classDef block present, all labels with special characters are double-quoted, no node id named 'end', all 11 nodes carry a class, node count 11 <= 14.

title: Context as protected memory (the Context MMU)
caption: The Context MMU: a tool result does not enter the model's context just because the call ran -- DOS adjudicates the RESULT at an admission gate. PASS bytes enter protected context; FAIL bytes are quarantined and fanned to headroom-compression (the page-in compressor, 40-80% tokens cut @ ~17 ms), dropped, or paged out to a side store the model can deliberately fault back in, where it must be re-admitted. One page table maps each context segment to its physical KV across HBM / DRAM / SSD / recompute / compressed -- 'what the model sees' and 'where its KV lives' are the same table.
altText: A top-down flowchart where a tool result flows into DOS (the Context MMU) and an admission gate; PASS results enter protected context, FAIL results are quarantined and fan out to headroom-compression, drop, or page-out to a side store that the model can deliberately fault back in through the gate, with a note that one page table maps context segments to HBM/DRAM/SSD/recompute/compressed.
killerNumber: headroom: 40-80% tokens cut @ ~17 ms
issuesFound: Faithfulness: verified all required elements present (result -> admission gate -> PASS/protected-context vs FAIL/quarantine fanning to compress/drop/page-out, page-table note with {HBM/DRAM/SSD/recompute/compressed}, and the killer number). No edits needed for content.

Style/clarity: reassigned the 'model / agent' node from class 'untrusted' to the previously-unused 'frontier' class -- the model deliberately faulting a page back in is the agent action, distinct from a raw untrusted tool result; this also exercises the defined frontier class. Kept 'result' as untrusted.

title: The KV cache is pageable: a memory hierarchy
caption: The KV cache as a pageable, four-tier memory hierarchy: because HBM (not compute) binds idle agents, KV is paged L1 HBM to L2 DRAM to L3 SSD to L4 recompute, with the L3-vs-L4 choice decided by transfer-vs-recompute cost. Per 80GB H100: 8B@50K holds ~10 warm agents, 3B ~42, headroom-compressed 3B ~100-150.
altText: A top-down flowchart showing KV cache paging from L1 HBM through L2 DRAM and L3 SSD into an L4 recompute backstop, with a crossover decision node routing to keep-bytes or recompute, and a density note listing agents-per-H100.
issuesFound: Verified init first line and classDef block match the required verbatim text exactly.
Confirmed all labels containing ( ) [ ] { } / , : are wrapped in double quotes; HTML entities (< >) used for angle/arrow chars inside labels to avoid parser issues.
Confirmed every node has a class assignment and no node id is named 'end'.

Tightened L4 'holds' label from 'nothing - the backstop, drop the KV' to 'the backstop - drop the KV, re-prefill' so the tier still describes what it does (the source frames L4 as the recompute backstop, not an empty tier).
Restored 'over PCIe/NVLink' detail to L2 medium label for full faithfulness to the source.
Verified all 4 tiers carry medium + make-resident cost + holds; crossover rule and density/killer-number note present. Node count is 9 (<=14), single clean top-down flow.
Tightened caption to two sentences naming the punchline and surfacing the killer density numbers.

title: Residency follows scheduler state
caption: KV residency is a pure function of scheduler state: RUNNABLE and a sub-millisecond BLOCKED_FAST call keep KV in HBM (L1), a slow network/sandbox/human BLOCKED_SLOW call pages it to DRAM (L2), and a long-idle SLEEPING steward drops to SSD (L3) or recompute (L4). The same static tool table that powers pre-flight denial also carries each tool's latency class, so paging and verification fall out of one mechanism, two payoffs.
altText: A left-to-right state machine showing RUNNABLE in HBM transitioning on fire-fast/fire-slow calls to BLOCKED_FAST (stays HBM) or BLOCKED_SLOW (DRAM L2), returning on resume, and on idle timeout to SLEEPING (SSD L3 or recompute L4), with a note reading one mechanism, two payoffs.
killerNumber: one mechanism, two payoffs
issuesFound: Renamed the FAST and SLOW state descriptions to surface the required state names BLOCKED_FAST and BLOCKED_SLOW (the 'Must include' state names were missing from the labels).

Re-mapped state classes to match each state's role: RUNNABLE and FAST -> kernel (both scheduler-active and resident in HBM/L1), SLOW -> mem (paged to DRAM/L2), SLEEP -> cheap (cheapest tier, SSD/recompute). The draft's RUNNABLE-as-frontier mapping did not reflect any role in the source.
Verified all required transitions are present: fire fast call, fire slow call, tool returns (resume) x2, idle timeout, plus an entry edge and a sleep-resume edge.
Confirmed the verbatim init block is the first line and the full classDef block is present unchanged.
Confirmed the killer number 'one mechanism, two payoffs' is present as a note-class node and every state node carries a class.
No invented facts: all tiers (L1 HBM, L2 DRAM, L3 SSD, L4 recompute), the <1ms fast threshold, and the token-log + shared-prefix-pointer detail match the source.

title: Shared-prefix KV: copy-on-write across the fleet
caption: A fleet boots from one common preamble, so its shared-prefix KV is computed once and held copy-on-write while each agent owns only a small divergent suffix; paging moves just that suffix, and the HBM saving grows with fleet size. A boundary steward keeps the shared prefix stopping exactly at the public edge so no agent-private tokens leak into a shared page.
altText: A diagram showing three agents all referencing one shared copy-on-write prefix KV block, each with its own divergent suffix that alone moves on paging, a boundary-steward gate preventing private tokens from leaking into shared pages, and a note that the HBM saving grows with fleet size.
killerNumber: HBM saving that GROWS with fleet size
issuesFound: Re-grounded the killer number: linked the 'HBM saving GROWS with fleet size' note to the three agents (a1/a2/a3 --> num) so the saving visibly scales with the many-agents fan-out, instead of hanging off only the single prefix node.
Made the paging relationship explicit by labeling the suffix-to-page edges 'page moves suffix only' (dashed), reinforcing that only the divergent tail travels while the prefix stays resident.

Verified faithfulness: all 'Must include' elements present (3 agents sharing ONE CoW prefix, computed once/resident, per-agent divergent suffix, paging moves only suffix, public/shared boundary caveat).

Verified syntax: init block is the exact required first line; full verbatim classDef block present; all 11 nodes assigned a class; labels containing ( ) + , : are double-quoted; no node id named 'end'; brackets balanced; 11 nodes total (<=14).
Tightened the caption to a 2-sentence punchline naming the copy-on-write share, suffix-only paging, fleet-scaling saving, and the boundary steward.

title: The 2-D scheduler: {which agent} x {which tier}
caption: A 2-D scheduler routes {which agent} x {which tier} across a compute hierarchy that mirrors the memory hierarchy: a tiny CPU/DRAM model handles stewards, pre-flight and vDSO-class adjudication, a small GPU model runs the agentic hot path, and a frontier GPU/HBM model handles only the few hard steps. The same syscall signal that pages memory promotes/demotes agents across model tiers -- the lever is routing each token to the cheapest tier that can do the step.
altText: A left-to-right flowchart showing a token-per-watt lever and 2-D scheduler feeding three compute tiers -- tiny CPU/DRAM, small GPU, frontier GPU/HBM -- with promote and demote arrows between them and a syscall signal driving the paging both ways.
killerNumber: route each token to the cheapest tier that can do the step

Relabeled CPU->GPU as 'promote back to GPU' and GPU->FRONTIER as 'promote: step needs the big model'.

Corrected the class mapping: the draft assigned both lever and sched to the note class. Kept the lever (the headline killer number) as note, and remapped sched (the scheduler/kernel concept) to the kernel class so the role->class mapping is meaningful.

title: 10x stewards: gardening by cheap single-invariant validators
caption: A supervisor tree of stewards: a Meta-steward oversees a population of single-invariant stewards on the cheap CPU/DRAM tier, each watching the Fleet for exactly one invariant. Every steward is fail-to-abstain -- it FIRES only with a witness it did not author, otherwise it ABSTAINS -- and firing stewards feed steering back to the Fleet (rebalance a lane, demote a draft class), while the Meta-steward prunes non-firing stewards and proposes new ones.
altText: A flowchart showing a Meta-steward at top overseeing three single-invariant stewards on the cheap tier that watch the Fleet, each routing through a witness gate to FIRES-with-witness or ABSTAINS, with firing stewards sending steering arrows back to the Fleet.
killerNumber: one steward per invariant, fail-to-abstain
issuesFound: Reworked the decision flow: in the draft the abstain/fire diamond read off the Fleet node, which muddled the semantics (the abstain-vs-fire choice belongs to a steward's own evaluation, not the Fleet's output). Now the steward population routes into the witness gate, which branches to FIRES or ABSTAINS, and only FIRES feeds steering back to the Fleet.
Made the steward->Fleet relationship explicitly 'watches' (dotted) instead of a plain solid arrow, so the watch (observe) link is visually distinct from the steering (act) link feeding back.

Collapsed the three separate meta->s1/s2/s3 prune arrows into one meta -.-> pop edge (and gave the subgraph 'pop' a cheap class), reducing edge clutter while keeping the prune/propose semantics, with the RSI-hook note moved into the meta label.
Tightened the meta label to fit and added '(RSI hook)' to make the meta-steward's tie to the recursive-self-improvement loop explicit per the source.
Assigned a class to every node including the previously unclassed 'pop' subgraph; renamed the diamond id from 'abstain' to 'gate' to avoid confusion and align it with the gate class.
Verified the killer number 'one steward per invariant / fail-to-abstain' is present as a note-class node, init line is verbatim, and the full classDef block is present verbatim.

title: RSI-first: the keep-or-revert loop
caption: RSI-first: the keep-or-revert loop. A candidate is PROPOSEd, APPLYed in an isolated git worktree, VERIFYed by a witness the author didn't write, and MEASUREd; it is KEPT (merged) only when the gate confirms a strictly-measured gain (suite green + truth syscall clean + strictly-higher metric), else REVERTed -- and a streak of non-keeps trips a breaker that ESCALATEs to a human.
altText: A left-to-right cycle from PROPOSE through APPLY, VERIFY, MEASURE into a gain-confirmed gate that either KEEPs (merges) back to the start or REVERTs into a breaker that escalates to a human on a non-keep streak, with the keep-rule shown as a headline note.
killerNumber: kept only if a witness the author didn't write confirms a measured gain
issuesFound: Verified init line matches the required verbatim block exactly (first line).
Verified the required classDef block is present verbatim and every node is assigned a class.
Confirmed the full cycle is present and closes: PROPOSE -> APPLY (isolated worktree) -> VERIFY (witness != author) -> MEASURE -> gate{gain confirmed?} -> KEEP (merge) back to PROPOSE, or REVERT.
Confirmed the non-keep streak path: REVERT -> breaker -> ESCALATE to human (with a 'not yet' return to PROPOSE).
Confirmed the killer number is surfaced as the headline via the note-class 'rule' node; faithful to source (uses the precise 'strictly-measured gain' wording).
Checked all labels with special characters (parentheses, !=, +, ?) are wrapped in double quotes; no node id named 'end'; brackets balanced; edge/label syntax valid. Diagram parses as-is.
No content changes needed for faithfulness; tightened the caption and added alt text. 10 nodes, single LR flow direction.

title: The honest numbers: real vs oversold
caption: The honest economics of the fused agent kernel: the left column is real and measured -- ~1000x off tool-call overhead, 100-10,000x on vDSO-class cached calls, ns-to-us in-process DOS adjudication, 40-80% context-token savings via headroom, and a ~53:1 input:output ratio proving the cost center is re-sent context, not generation. The right column is honest about what is gated: only ~2-2.5x token-per-watt vs a TUNED baseline, always-on density HBM-bound to ~10-40 sessions/GPU, and a headline token-per-watt KPI you cannot even measure until nvml/RAPL is instrumented. Punchline: ~1000x on overhead is real; '100x always-on' is HBM-gated.
altText: A two-column flowchart contrasting real, measured kernel wins (1000x overhead collapse, 100-10,000x vDSO, ns-us adjudication, 40-80% headroom, 53:1 context ratio) against gated or oversold claims (2x not 100x vs a tuned baseline, 10-40 sessions/GPU HBM limit, token-per-watt unmeasurable today), with a build-order note and a killer-number node.
killerNumber: ~1000x on overhead is real; '100x always-on' is HBM-gated
issuesFound: Verified all required numbers are present and faithful: overhead ~1000x [med-high], vDSO 100-10,000x [high], DOS adjudication 38ns-160us in-process [measured], headroom 40-80% at ~17ms, the ~53:1 (150,168 in : 2,817 out) ~98% re-sent ratio; and the gated side: ~2-2.5x vs TUNED baseline (5-15x vs naive), HBM-bound ~10-40 sessions/GPU, token-per-watt unmeasurable today (instrument nvml/RAPL first).
Confirmed the killer number appears verbatim as a note-class node (KILLER): '~1000x on overhead is REAL; 100x always-on is HBM-GATED'.
Confirmed the build-order note (ORDER) lists all five steps in order: watts first, resident-KV + tool-wait batching, vDSO + pre-flight, headroom as admit gate, speculation last.

Clarity: 10 nodes (<=14 limit), single LR flow into a shared KILLER note then the ORDER note; tightened the caption to plain English with the punchline named. No structural changes were needed beyond verification and caption polish.

title: The net-gain frontier: when the build repays
caption: The net-gain frontier for the dual-path inline / fused build (one-time ~$200k). Each plotted point is a break-even run count from inline_tool_roi.py ($ saved/run = N*miss*P/3000), so the frontier is the hyperbola N*miss*P = const -- an anti-diagonal in this log-log map. You must clear it on the PRODUCT: pressure alone (N=1 on a cold 128k context -> 23,438 runs) never lands, scale alone (N=1k but warm-KV / 8k context -> 7,500 runs) never lands, only fleet scale x re-prefill pressure together reaches NET GAIN (N>=1k with cold long context -> 2-23 runs). Inline / fusion is a scale optimization, exactly as the analysis predicts.
altText: A quadrant chart of the net-gain frontier for the inline/fused build: x-axis is fleet scale (N=1 to 10k), y-axis is re-prefill pressure (miss-rate times context). Top-right is green NET GAIN (repays in 2-25 runs), bottom-left is red NET LOSS (never repays), and the two off-diagonal quadrants are gold transition bands (scale-limited and pressure-limited). Six operating points carry break-even run counts from 23,438 (N=1) down to 2 (N=10k, or N=1k on a 512k context).
killerNumber: N=1 never repays (23,438 runs); N=10k repays in 2 -- only with cold-KV pressure
issuesFound: Added after the original 14-figure batch to cover the newest analysis (inline_tool_roi.py, 18:23). This is the deck's first quadrantChart, not a flowchart -- so it cannot carry the shared classDef vocabulary; the deck palette is preserved instead via quadrant fills (quadrant1 = pass-green for NET GAIN, quadrant3 = deny-red for NET LOSS, quadrant2/4 = gate-gold for the frontier band) and the kernel-blue point fill, all set in the init themeVariables block.

Faithfulness: every plotted break-even count is recomputed from inline_tool_roi.py (BUILD_COST=$200k; saved/run = N*K*miss*P/rho_pre/3600*gpus*price = N*miss*P/3000 at the script's constants) -- N=1 -> 23,438; N=100 -> 234; N=1k -> 23; N=10k -> 2 (all at miss=20%, P=128k, matching the script's fleet-scaling and break-even tables). The two vertical-sweep points re-derive the same way: N=1k warm-KV (P=8k, miss=1%) -> 7,500; N=1k 512k-ctx (miss=50%) -> 2.

Design: a horizontal sweep (vary N at fixed pressure) crosses a vertical sweep (vary pressure at fixed N=1k) so the figure shows BOTH axes are necessary -- the frontier is the product N*miss*P, not either factor alone (the one point the thesis and the COUNTER agree on).

Syntax: renders cleanly under mermaid-cli 11.15.0; replaced an HTML-escaping '<=25' with the plain range '2-25 runs', shortened the title so it no longer clips the chart box, and tightened the two off-diagonal quadrant labels so centered text stays inside its quadrant. PNG visually inspected at 2x.

Scope honesty: the gold quadrants are a marginal / transition band, not strict loss -- e.g. N=100 (234 runs) does eventually repay; the caption frames clearing the frontier as a product condition, not a hard pass/fail line.

title: What survived contact with data
caption: The 2026-06-16/17 empirical audit of the cluster's top-5 load-bearing claims, each prototyped + tested against real telemetry and fresh microbenchmarks and adversarially verified. KV-density gate CONFIRMED (reproduces from real HF GQA math). Fusion multiplier REFINED DOWN to ~10x vs Go-spawn / ~130x vs Python-spawn (the claimed ~100x/~2500x priced the fused side at a 5ns floor the real hook never hits). Cross-agent dedup REFINED DOWN to 1.105x (sharing is 1.8% of keys; prefix caching already harvests ~94%). I:O ratio MEASURED HIGHER at 239:1 (median session 382:1) than the cited 53:1. Speculation hit-rate REFINED DOWN to ~29% side-effect-safe yield. Net: the qualitative direction of all 5 held, but 4/5 magnitudes were overstated and I:O understated; 0/29 claims scored NOVEL, so the IP is the assembly of known parts.
altText: A five-row flowchart, one row per claim, each showing the claim then its claimed value then its measured value and verdict: KV-density confirmed (green), fusion multiplier / dedup / speculation refined down (red), I:O ratio measured higher (blue), with a net summary note that all directions held but most magnitudes were overstated and 0/29 claims were novel.
killerNumber: 5/5 directions held, 4/5 magnitudes overstated, 0/29 NOVEL
issuesFound: Every number traces to fleet-cluster-empirical-findings: KV-density 8B@50K=10 / 3B@50K=43; fusion ~100x/~2500x claimed vs ~10x/~130x measured; dedup 'approaches N' claimed vs 1.105x measured (1.8% of keys); I:O cited 53:1 vs measured 239:1 (median 382:1); speculation raw 49% vs safe yield ~29%; 0/29 claims NOVEL.

Color encodes verdict: KV-density pass (green, confirmed), I:O kernel (blue, measured higher not worse), the three refined-down claims deny (red). Claim labels untrusted (amber), claimed values gate (gold).
Renders cleanly under mermaid-cli 11.15.0; PNG visually inspected at 2x. Disconnected three-node chains stack vertically as intended.

title: Sound, not complete, not precise
caption: The context-MMU 'uninjectable context' write-time admission gate (the repo's flagship security benchmark), run on our own session transcript. It is SOUND (0 trigger-bytes leaked after quarantine, independently re-derived) but neither COMPLETE (42 obfuscated payloads -> approximately 100% allowed; 24/24 agent red-team + 16/18 canonical battery) nor PRECISE (on our own context 4/4 quarantines were false positives -- it held mmu.go, ctxmmu_test.go, and poison.json out of context, the gate reading its own source). Root cause: detection is regex (secrets) + ASCII substring (markers) with no NFKC normalization, no decode, no provenance, no semantics -- a T1-class classifier. Cheap fix: normalize + strip zero-width before match, decode-and-rescan high-entropy blobs, and gate quarantine on untrusted egress rather than trusted local file reads.
altText: A flowchart from the ctxmmu.Admit gate branching into three property nodes -- soundness (green, holds, zero leak), completeness (red, fails, approximately 100% evaded), precision (red, fails, 4/4 false positives) -- the two failures pointing to a root-cause note (regex plus substring, a T1 classifier) which points to a gold fix node.
killerNumber: sound (0 leak) but ~100% evaded + 4/4 false positives
issuesFound: Numbers traced to a private transcript-derived benchmark: 0-leak soundness; approximately 100% evasion across 42 payloads; 4/4 false-positive census. The fix list follows the private report's recommendations.
Color: gate kernel-blue, soundness pass-green, the two failure modes deny-red, root-cause note-grey, fix gold (an actionable decision). Distinguishes 'architecture sound' from 'detection bound'.
Renders cleanly under mermaid-cli 11.15.0; PNG visually inspected at 2x.

title: The evasion battery
caption: An xychart of the adversarial evasion run against ctxmmu.Admit. Across 42 obfuscated payloads (24 from an independent agent red-team, 18 canonical) every SOTA-cited obfuscation family -- character spacing, base64/hex encoding, Unicode homoglyph/fullwidth, zero-width/bidi, pure paraphrase, cross-result split, and secret-format variants -- walked straight into context at approximately 100%; only the verbatim controls were caught. The green reference line marks the 5%-evasion 'failing grade' floor that every obfuscation bar towers over, matching the COMPARE doc's cited 'evadable up to 100%' for a T1 detector.
altText: A bar chart with seven obfuscation-family bars all at 100% evaded and a single control bar at 0%, with a green horizontal reference line near 5% marking the security failing-grade floor that every obfuscation bar towers over.
killerNumber: ~100% of 42 obfuscated payloads evaded the gate
issuesFound: Bar heights reflect the report's per-family verdicts (sec 5 table + sec 8c): every obfuscation family EVADED, controls caught. Aggregate approximately 100% over 42 payloads is the combined headline (sec 8c).

The reference line was corrected from 95 to 5: on an evasion-rate y-axis the security 'failing grade' is <5% evasion (you need >95% catch), so 5 is the meaningful floor every bar exceeds. Title shortened so it no longer clips the chart box.

Minor: long x-axis category labels abut slightly (mermaid xychart-beta limitation, same as figs 17/19); labels remain readable. Renders cleanly under mermaid-cli 11.15.0; PNG visually inspected at 2x.

title: A finished session is a core dump
caption: The recall leaf's persist/reload path. Because the context-MMU already pages tool results to a content-addressed store at write time, recall.Recorder.Persist writes a core image: manifest.json (the page table -- roles, digests, a real extractive descriptor, and the quarantine state) plus cas.json (the swap device of content-addressed bytes). recall.Load(dir) rebuilds a Session with its OWN CAS and a FRESH ctxmmu gate, verifying every blob against its digest so a tampered device fails closed. The load-bearing property: the quarantine seal outlives the process boundary -- a page sealed before the boundary stays sealed after it. recall makes the gate's decision durable and re-screenable; it does not change the decision.
altText: A left-to-right flow: a live session streams results into the ctxmmu gate, which pages them to a core image (manifest.json page table plus cas.json swap device) inside a subgraph; a process-boundary node marks the session ending as a core dump; recall.Load rebuilds a fresh self-contained Session; a note states the seal outlives the process.
killerNumber: the quarantine seal outlives the process boundary
issuesFound: Faithful to RECALL-RESULTS.md and the fak-recall-leaf-shipped memory: Persist writes manifest.json + cas.json; Load rebuilds with its own CAS + a fresh gate and integrity-checks Digest(b)==key (tamper fails closed); the seal survives persist->reload.
Honest framing carried in the note: recall makes the decision durable + re-screenable, it does NOT improve the decision (it inherits ctxmmu's evadable detector).

Color: live session untrusted (amber), gate + load kernel-blue, the two on-disk artifacts mem-violet (a memory tier / side store), boundary deny-red, rebuilt session pass-green. Renders cleanly under mermaid-cli 11.15.0; PNG visually inspected at 2x.

title: The re-admit ladder (clearance does not launder poison)
caption: The Resolve ladder in a reloaded recall Session. Rung 0: a benign page re-outputs byte-identical at zero model tokens. Rung 2: Recall(query,k) demand-pages a top-k working set, and quarantined pages are never candidates. Rung 4 -- the moat: a quarantined page is REFUSED unless a witness Clear() ran AND the bytes pass a fresh ctxmmu.Admit re-screen; if no witness it is sealed, and if cleared-but-still-flagged it is refused with 'clearance does not launder poison'. Only a genuinely-benign cleared page is released. Clearance is necessary-but-not-sufficient, and the re-screen is the precise lever a tightened pattern set pulls to re-catch an evasion that looked benign at write time. A 5-skeptic default-REFUTED panel confirmed the moat 5/5.
altText: A top-down decision tree: Resolve branches by benign-or-quarantined; benign rung-0 and Recall rung-2 lead to green release paths; the quarantined rung-4 moat asks whether a witness Clear ran (no -> refused), then whether a fresh re-screen passes (no -> refused 'clearance does not launder poison', yes -> released), with a note that clearance is necessary but not sufficient.
killerNumber: clearance does not launder poison (5/5 confirmed)
issuesFound: Faithful to RECALL-RESULTS.md: the two refusal messages are the report's verbatim strings; rungs 0/2/4 match the shipped Resolve ladder; quarantined pages excluded from Recall candidates; 5/5 default-REFUTED skeptics confirmed.

Color: query untrusted (amber), the three decision diamonds gate-gold, the release/byte-identical/working-set outcomes pass-green, the two refusals deny-red, the necessary-not-sufficient explainer note-grey.
Renders cleanly under mermaid-cli 11.15.0; PNG visually inspected at 2x; all labels with parentheses/slashes are quoted.

title: Machine facts from the transcripts
caption: A dashboard of the headline facts from the session-transcript audit, all from the exact message.usage token records (the only place exact counts live). Context economics: I:O ratio 239:1 machine-wide (median session 382:1) vs the cluster's cited 53:1, so tool-call time approximately equals context-ingestion time; and approximately 94% of ingested context is cache-read (intra-session prompt-cache reuse), so the KV-reuse win is largely already harvested. Coverage and provenance: the true log floor is 2026-05-13 (about 34 days; no pre-2026 / 8-month data exists anywhere), and subagents write separate transcripts so top-level sessions undercount real work by about 32%. One-month trend: Opus 4.7->4.8 migrated at the W22/W23 boundary (early June) with Fable-5 entering W24, and amid about 7x volume growth the cache-hit rate eroded 96.7% to 92.6%.
altText: A dashboard flowchart with a source node (exact token counts from message.usage) feeding three labeled groups: context economics (I:O 239:1, ~94% cache-read), coverage and provenance (log floor 2026-05-13, subagents undercount ~32%), and one-month trend (Opus 4.7->4.8 migration, ~7x growth with cache-hit eroding 96.7%->92.6%).
killerNumber: I:O 239:1, ~94% cache-read, log floor 2026-05-13
issuesFound: Every figure traces to session-transcript-audit-method and fleet-cluster-empirical-findings: I:O 239:1 (median 382:1) vs cited 53:1; ~94% cache-read; log floor 2026-05-13 (~34 days, no 8-month data); subagents undercount ~32%; Opus 4.7->4.8 at W22/W23; ~7x growth; cache-hit 96.7%->92.6%.
Color: source kernel-blue, context-economics mem-violet (a memory/KV story), coverage/provenance cheap-slate (bookkeeping), the trend note-grey. Three subgraphs group the facts by theme.
Renders cleanly under mermaid-cli 11.15.0; PNG visually inspected at 2x.
